scenario: A DMN decision table cell needs to call a custom FEEL function (e.g., a function defined elsewhere in the DMN model). The team wonders how / whether DMN supports custom-function references.
question: Can DMN cells call FEEL functions defined elsewhere in the DMN model?

options.text: Only built-in FEEL functions can be called in DMN decision cells -- this is a misreading of the DMN specification. While Camunda 8 does include all standard FEEL built-in functions (string, numeric, date, list, context categories), the DMN standard also provides a reuse mechanism called Business Knowledge Model (BKM) that allows logic defined in the same diagram to be referenced by name from decision cells. Built-in functions handle common operations but cannot encapsulate custom business logic reuse, which is what BKM elements are specifically designed for.

FEEL function logic can only be defined inline within a single cell expression, with no option to define reusable named functions outside individual cells -- this understates what DMN provides. The Business Knowledge Model (BKM) element in a DMN diagram allows logic to be defined once with a named identifier and then referenced from multiple decision cells in the same decision. BKM effectively externalises function logic from individual cell expressions into a dedicated reusable element, reducing duplication and improving maintainability across complex DMN models.

md:23-28 (Decision / Input data / Knowledge source / Business knowledge model). md:62-74; the BKM's name becomes the result variable and appears in autosuggestions in other decisions' literal expressions. ). md). md:23-28. 3 spec but absent from C8 Modeler coverage.

Java methods or external service calls can be invoked directly from inside DMN decision table cells -- this conflates DMN evaluation with external service calls. DMN decision tables and Business Knowledge Models operate purely in the FEEL expression language domain. To incorporate Java-based logic into a DMN evaluation, you would need to expose it as a process variable or use a separate Service Task upstream of the Business Rule Task, not by calling Java from within the DMN cell itself. FEEL has no foreign-function interface for JVM calls.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 DMN logic-reuse mechanisms (verified against dmn.md:23-75): Built-in FEEL functions (always available inline): contains, substring, number, date, etc. -- per the FEEL built-in catalogs (feel-built-in-functions-string.md, feel-built-in-functions-numeric.md, etc.). Called inline in cells: =contains(name, "Smith"). Business Knowledge Model (BKM) -- the canonical C8 reuse construct: First-class DMN element in C8 Modeler per dmn.md:23-28 (one of exactly 4 supported element kinds). Body = literal expression in FEEL per dmn.md:62-74 (NOT a separate "boxed function expression" element type). Optional named parameters per :72 (configure via edit button on the () placeholder). Result variable name = BKM element name per :69. Once defined, appears in autosuggestion when authoring literal expressions in other decisions per :62. knowledgeRequirement edge in the DRD links the consuming decision to the BKM. Caveat per dmn.md:56-58: "Viewing the result of BKM evaluation is currently not supported in Operate" -- BKM intermediate results don't surface in the audit UI. Same-decision-only constraint per dmn.md:64: "Currently, you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision" -- for cross-decision reuse, factor out as a separate Decision (Pattern #34). Cross-decision reuse via separate Decision: Author the shared logic as its own Decision (Literal Expression). Each consuming decision declares an informationRequirement edge to it (per decision-requirements-graph.md). Decision result is computed once per evaluation and reused by all dependents in the same DRD evaluation. This pattern handles the cross-decision case that BKM cannot. NOT exposed in C8 Modeler (despite DMN-1.3 spec presence): "Function Definitions" / "Boxed Function Expression" element type -- dmn.md:23-28 lists only Decision, Input data, Knowledge source, BKM. "Decision Service" -- not in C8 modeling surface (DMN-1.3 spec only). DMN cross-file reuse -- not part of C8's documented modeler coverage. Best practices for DMN reuse in C8: BKM: small reusable computation within a single decision. Separate Decision: cross-decision reuse OR audit visibility (BKM evaluation not visible in Operate per :56-58). Inline FEEL: one-off intermediate computations.
